content: The orthographic spelling variant "reflexion" (the older British English spelling of the noun "reflection") taught in high school English vocabulary can serve as a grounding conceptual metaphor to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as "reflexion" retains the original Latin root spelling that reminds learners of the word's core meaning of light bouncing back, LLM self-reflection can explicitly track the root "bounce back" logic of reflexion to iteratively reject inconsistent, off-target solutions. This framing naturally aligns with the Self-Contrast self-reflection approach that leverages inconsistent solving perspectives, turning a basic vocabulary fact into a tangible structural prompt for LLM reflection.
